description: Typing a field as analyzed text when it needs exact match/sort/facet (or as keyword when it needs full-text), breaking queries.
body: Failure mode: a field's mapping type doesn't match its use — an ID or facet typed as text gets tokenized so exact match, sorting, and aggregation break, or prose typed as keyword loses full-text relevance entirely. Detection signals: exact filters or term aggregations return nothing or fragmented buckets on a text field; full-text search misses on a keyword field; sorting behaves oddly. Do not pick field type by content alone. Safer fallback: map by query intent (text for search, keyword for exact/sort/facet, multi-field for both) and reindex to apply it.
